poj: I sī khì Tâi-pak, sī bô·?
poj_sandhi: I7 sī3 khì2 Tâi7-pak, sī bô·3?
english: He went to Taipei, didn't he?
I like to write with a fountain pen.

poj: Che sī góa ê chheh, m̄-sī lí ê.
poj_sandhi: Che7 sī3 góa1 ê7 chheh, m̄3-sī3 lí2 ê.
taiwanese_hanji: 這是我的冊, 毋是你的.
english: This is my book, not yours.

poj: Góa chin bô ài goán-chú-pit siá-jī.
poj_sandhi: Góa1 chin7 bô7 ài2 goán1-chú1-pit siá1-jī.
english: I hate writing with ballpoint pens.

poj: Góa ê chheh hō· lí khòaⁿ.
poj_sandhi: Góa1 ê7 chheh hō·3 lí1 khòaⁿ.
english: I'm giving you my book to read.

poj: I boeh khì, góa iā boeh khì.
poj_sandhi: I7 boeh2 khì, góa1 iā3 boeh2 khì.
english: He's going and so am I.

poj: I bô boeh khì, góa iā bô boeh khì.
poj_sandhi: I7 bô7 boeh2 khì, góa1 iā3 bô7 boeh2 khì.
taiwanese_hanji: 伊無欲去, 我也無欲去.
english: He's not going; I'm not either.

poj: I boeh khì, góa iā m̄ boeh khì.
poj_sandhi: I7 boeh2 khì, góa1 iā3 m̄3 boeh2 khì.
taiwanese_hanji: 伊欲去, 我也毋欲去.
english: Even if he goes, I'm not going.
Students must use a pencil to write with.
He came to Taichung to study.
No, I don't want to go.
What are you saying?/What are you talking about?

poj: Lín ha̍k-seng siaⁿ-lâng boeh khì Tâi-pak?
poj_sandhi: Lín1 ha̍k3-seng siaⁿ7-lâng boeh2 khì2 Tâi7-pak?
english: Which one of you students is going to Taipei?
